_comment: Platform/deployment constants for ai-transport, owned by Platform Governance (Vlad) per ADR-022 §10 Policy Ownership. No Process may modify its own Policy. Introduced by ADR-030 (Controlled Full-Mesh Transport Access).
max_hops_rationale: ADR-030 §3.2: deliberately NOT derived from current participant count (5). Independent platform constant, tunable without redefining loop-protection every time Orchestra gains or loses a participant.

circuit_breaker.on_trigger: Suspend all new writes platform-wide, notify Vlad. Provisional values (ADR-030 §3.5) — not yet tuned against real usage.
circuit_breaker.enforcement_note: No automated monitor currently reads this file. Enforcement requires either a GitHub Action or an n8n workflow watching write volume; neither exists yet. Until one does, this section is a declared intent, not a live control.

dead_letter_reasons.SCHEMA_INVALID: write failed schemas/mission.schema.json validation at commit time (ADR-030 §3.4)

participants_with_physical_access.Grok: GitHub App (scoped to this repo) - LIVE since 2026-07-01
participants_with_physical_access.ChatGPT: STRUCTURALLY LIMITED - native connector is READ-ONLY (write requires Codex, a separate product). See PERP research 2026-07-01.
participants_with_physical_access.Gemini: NOT AVAILABLE on consumer tier - no persistent connector, only ephemeral single-chat repo import (5000 files/100MB cap). Persistent connector exists only in Gemini Enterprise (separate Google Cloud product). See PERP research 2026-07-01.
participants_with_physical_access.Perplexity: NOT AVAILABLE on current (Free) tier - GitHub connector is a Pro/Max/Enterprise feature, absent from Connectors settings on Free. See PERP research 2026-07-01.

access_note: Revised 2026-07-01 after Perplexity research: the ADR-030 assumption of symmetric full-mesh access across all 5 participants does NOT hold natively for ChatGPT and Gemini regardless of provisioning effort - these are product-level constraints (read-only connector / no persistent connector on consumer tier), not a 'not yet configured' gap. Perplexity's gap is tier-based (Free lacks the feature; Pro/Max/Enterprise reportedly has it, exact repo-scoping unconfirmed in public docs). Closing these gaps requires either a different product/environment per model (Codex for ChatGPT write access, Gemini Enterprise for Gemini, a paid tier for Perplexity) or a custom bridge (n8n/API) built and maintained by us - not a settings toggle.
